  1015→
  1016→    def _eval(self, node) -> Any:
  1017→        if isinstance(node, ast.Constant):
  1018→            return node.value
  1019→        if isinstance(node, ast.Num):
  1020→            return node.n
  1021→        if isinstance(node, ast.Str):
  1022→            return node.s
  1023→        if isinstance(node, ast.NameConstant):
  1024→            return node.value
  1025→
  1026→        if isinstance(node, ast.Name):
  1027→            col = node.id
  1028→            val = self.df.at[self.row_idx, col]
  1029→            if isinstance(val, pd.Timestamp):
  1030→                return val.to_pydatetime()
  1031→            return val
  1032→
  1033→        if isinstance(node, ast.UnaryOp):
  1034→            operand = self._eval(node.operand)
  1035→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Not):
  1036→                return not operand
  1037→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.USub):
  1038→                return -operand
  1039→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.UAdd):
  1040→                return +operand
  1041→            raise TypeError(f"Unknown unary op: {node.op}")
  1042→
  1043→        if isinstance(node, ast.BinOp):
  1044→            left = self._eval(node.left)
  1045→            right = self._eval(node.right)
  1046→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Add):
  1047→                return left + right
  1048→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Sub):
  1049→                return left - right
  1050→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Mult):
  1051→                return left * right
  1052→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Div):
  1053→                if right == 0:
  1054→                    raise ZeroDivisionError("Division by zero")
  1055→                return left / right
  1056→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Mod):
  1057→                return left % right
  1058→            raise TypeError(f"Unknown binop: {node.op}")
  1059→
  1060→        if isinstance(node, ast.Compare):
  1061→            left = self._eval(node.left)
  1062→            for op, comparator in zip(node.ops, node.comparators):
  1063→                right = self._eval(comparator)
  1064→                if isinstance(op, ast.Eq):
  1065→                    if not (left == right):
  1066→                        return False
  1067→                elif isinstance(op, ast.NotEq):
  1068→                    if not (left != right):
  1069→                        return False
  1070→                elif isinstance(op, ast.Lt):
  1071→                    if not (left < right):
  1072→                        return False
  1073→                elif isinstance(op, ast.LtE):
  1074→                    if not (left <= right):
  1075→                        return False
  1076→                elif isinstance(op, ast.Gt):
  1077→                    if not (left > right):
  1078→                        return False
  1079→                elif isinstance(op, ast.GtE):
  1080→                    if not (left >= right):
  1081→                        return False
  1082→                else:
  1083→                    raise TypeError(f"Unknown compare op: {op}")
  1084→                left = right
  1085→            return True
  1086→
  1087→        if isinstance(node, ast.BoolOp):
  1088→            values = [self._eval(v) for v in node.values]
  1089→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.And):
  1090→                return all(values)
  1091→            if isinstance(node.op, ast.Or):
  1092→                return any(values)
  1093→            raise TypeError(f"Unknown boolop: {node.op}")
  1094→
  1095→        if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
  1096→            fn = node.func.id
  1097→            args = [self._eval(arg) for arg in node.args]
  1098→
  1099→            if fn == 'len':
  1100→                return len(args[0])
  1101→            if fn == 'day':
  1102→                return args[0].day
  1103→            if fn == 'month':
  1104→                return args[0].month
  1105→            if fn == 'year':
  1106→                return args[0].year
  1107→            if fn == 'round':
  1108→                val, digits = args[0], (args[1] if len(args) > 1 else 0)
  1109→                return round(val, digits)
  1110→            if fn == 'CONTAINS':
  1111→                return args[1] in args[0]
  1112→            if fn == 'PARAM':
  1113→                param_name = args[0]
  1114→                if param_name not in self.params:
  1115→                    raise KeyError(f"Unknown param: {param_name}")
  1116→                return self.params[param_name]
  1117→            if fn == 'COALESCE':
  1118→                for arg in args:
  1119→                    if arg is not None:
  1120→                        # For numeric, check if it's not NaN
  1121→                        if isinstance(arg, (int, float)) and np.isnan(arg):
  1122→                            continue
  1123→                        return arg
  1124→                return None
  1125→            if fn == 'CAST':
  1126→                val, type_str = args
  1127→                # No-op for same type
  1128→                return val
  1129→            if fn in ('starts_with', 'ends_with', 'contains'):
  1130→                str_val, pattern = args
  1131→                if fn == 'starts_with':
  1132→                    return str(str_val).startswith(str(pattern))
  1133→                elif fn == 'ends_with':
  1134→                    return str(str_val).endswith(str(pattern))
  1135→                elif fn == 'contains':
  1136→                    return str(pattern) in str(str_val)
  1137→
  1138→            raise TypeError(f"Unknown function: {fn}")
  1139→
  1140→        raise TypeError(f"Cannot evaluate: {type(node).__name__}")

  1145→class PostAggEvaluator:
  1146→    """Evaluate post-aggregation expressions over result rows."""
  1147→
  1148→    def __init__(self, results: List[Dict], params: Dict[str, Any], calcs: List[CalcInfo]):
  1149→        self.results = results
  1150→        self.params = params
  1151→        self.calcs = {c.name: c for c in calcs if c.stage == 'post_agg'}
  1152→        # Precompute all values
  1153→        self._computed = {}
  1154→
  1155→    def evaluate_all(self):
  1156→        """Evaluate all post-agg calcs for all result rows."""
  1157→        # First, add aggregate aliases and group keys to result rows
  1158→        for i, row in enumerate(self.results):
  1159→            self._computed[i] = {}
  1160→
  1161→        # Evaluate each post-agg calc in order
  1162→        for calc in self.calcs.values():
  1163→            for i, row in enumerate(self.results):
  1164→                try:
  1165→                    evaluator = PostAggRowEvaluator(row, self.params, self._computed)
  1166→                    val = evaluator.evaluate(calc.parsed_ast)
  1167→                    self._computed[i][calc.name] = val
  1168→                except Exception as e:
  1169→                    self._computed[i][calc.name] = None
  1170→
  1171→        # Add computed values to results
  1172→        for i, row in enumerate(self.results):
  1173→            for calc_name in self.calcs:
  1174→                row[calc_name] = self._computed[i][calc_name]
  1175→
  1176→        return self.results

  1357→    def _load_csv(self, path: str) -> pd.DataFrame:

  1382→    def resolve_schema(self, schema_name: str, all_schemas: Dict[str, Schema]) -> Dict[str, SchemaField]:
  1383→        """Resolve schema inheritance to get final field definitions."""
  1384→        if schema_name not in all_schemas:
  1385→            raise SyntaxError(f"Unknown schema: {schema_name}")
  1386→
  1387→        schema = all_schemas[schema_name]
  1388→
  1389→        # Check for cycles
  1390→        visited = set()
  1391→        current = schema_name
  1392→        while current:
  1393→            if current in visited:
  1394→                raise SyntaxError(f"Circular schema inheritance detected involving {current}")
  1395→            visited.add(current)
  1396→            if current not in all_schemas:
  1397→                break
  1398→            current_schema = all_schemas[current]
  1399→            if current_schema.base:
  1400→                current = current_schema.base
  1401→            else:
  1402→                break
  1403→
  1404→        # Build field map by walking inheritance chain
  1405→        fields = {}
  1406→        seen_names = set()
  1407→
  1408→        # Walk from base to derived (inheritance order)
  1409→        inheritance_chain = []
  1410→        current = schema_name
  1411→        while current:
  1412→            inheritance_chain.append(current)
  1413→            if current not in all_schemas:
  1414→                break
  1415→            base = all_schemas[current].base
  1416→            if base:
  1417→                if base not in all_schemas:
  1418→                    raise SyntaxError(f"Base schema not found: {base}")
  1419→                current = base
  1420→            else:
  1421→                break
  1422→        inheritance_chain.reverse()  # Base first
  1423→
  1424→        for schema_name_in_chain in inheritance_chain:
  1425→            sch = all_schemas[schema_name_in_chain]
  1426→            for field_name, field_def in sch.fields.items():
  1427→                if field_name in seen_names:
  1428→                    # Override: check if type change is legal
  1429→                    existing = fields[field_name]
  1430→                    if field_def.mtype != existing.mtype:
  1431→                        # Check if it's numeric widening
  1432→                        if not existing.mtype.can_widen_to(field_def.mtype):
  1433→                            raise TypeError(
  1434→                                f"Illegal override of {field_name}: "
  1435→                                f"{existing.mtype} -> {field_def.mtype} (narrowing not allowed)"
  1436→                            )
  1437→                    # Type is same or widened - update field info
  1438→                    fields[field_name] = field_def
  1439→                else:
  1440→                    fields[field_name] = field_def
  1441→                    seen_names.add(field_name)
  1442→
  1443→        return fields

  1447→        csv_cols = set(df.columns)
  1448→        for name, field in fields.items():
  1449→            if field.expr is None and name not in csv_cols:
  1450→                raise SyntaxError(f"Physical column '{name}' not found in CSV")
  1451→
  1452→    def resolve_param_values(self, params: List[ParamInfo]) -> Dict[str, Tuple[MTLType, Any]]:
  1453→        """Resolve parameter values from CLI or DSL defaults."""
  1454→        resolved = {}
  1455→
  1456→        for param in params:
  1457→            if param.name in self.param_values:
  1458→                # CLI value takes precedence
  1459→                value_str = self.param_values[param.name]
  1460→                ptype = param.mtype
  1461→                if ptype is None:
  1462→                    # Infer string type
  1463→                    value = value_str
  1464→                    ptype = MTLType.STRING
  1465→                else:
  1466→                    # Parse according to declared type
  1467→                    if ptype == MTLType.STRING:
  1468→                        value = value_str
  1469→                    elif ptype == MTLType.INT:
  1470→                        try:
  1471→                            value = int(value_str)
  1472→                        except ValueError:
  1473→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': cannot parse '{value_str}' as int")
  1474→                    elif ptype == MTLType.FLOAT:
  1475→                        try:
  1476→                            value = float(value_str)
  1477→                        except ValueError:
  1478→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': cannot parse '{value_str}' as float")
  1479→                    elif ptype == MTLType.BOOL:
  1480→                        if value_str.lower() in ('true', '1', 'yes', 'on'):
  1481→                            value = True
  1482→                        elif value_str.lower() in ('false', '0', 'no', 'off'):
  1483→                            value = False
  1484→                        else:
  1485→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': cannot parse '{value_str}' as bool")
  1486→                    elif ptype == MTLType.TIMESTAMP:
  1487→                        try:
  1488→                            # Try parsing as ISO or custom format
  1489→                            if self.timestamp_format:
  1490→                                value = datetime.strptime(value_str, self.timestamp_format)
  1491→                            else:
  1492→                                value = pd.to_datetime(value_str).to_pydatetime()
  1493→                        except Exception:
  1494→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': cannot parse '{value_str}' as timestamp")
  1495→                    else:
  1496→                        value = value_str
  1497→                resolved[param.name] = (ptype, value)
  1498→            elif param.default is not None:
  1499→                # Use DSL default - need to parse as literal
  1500→                default_str = param.default.strip()
  1501→                ptype = param.mtype
  1502→                if ptype is None:
  1503→                    ptype = MTLType.STRING
  1504→                    value = default_str.strip("'")  # Remove quotes
  1505→                else:
  1506→                    if ptype == MTLType.STRING:
  1507→                        value = default_str.strip("'")
  1508→                    elif ptype == MTLType.INT:
  1509→                        try:
  1510→                            value = int(default_str)
  1511→                        except ValueError:
  1512→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': default '{default_str}' not valid int")
  1513→                    elif ptype == MTLType.FLOAT:
  1514→                        try:
  1515→                            value = float(default_str)
  1516→                        except ValueError:
  1517→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': default '{default_str}' not valid float")
  1518→                    elif ptype == MTLType.BOOL:
  1519→                        if default_str.lower() == 'true':
  1520→                            value = True
  1521→                        elif default_str.lower() == 'false':
  1522→                            value = False
  1523→                        else:
  1524→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': default '{default_str}' not valid bool")
  1525→                    elif ptype == MTLType.TIMESTAMP:
  1526→                        try:
  1527→                            if self.timestamp_format:
  1528→                                value = datetime.strptime(default_str, self.timestamp_format)
  1529→                            else:
  1530→                                value = pd.to_datetime(default_str.strip("'")).to_pydatetime()
  1531→                        except Exception:
  1532→                            raise TypeError(f"Param '{param.name}': default '{default_str}' not valid timestamp")
  1533→                    else:
  1534→                        value = default_str
  1535→                resolved[param.name] = (ptype, value)
  1536→            else:
  1537→                raise SyntaxError(f"Param '{param.name}' must be provided via --param (no default)")
  1538→
  1539→        return resolved

  1541→    def get_column_info(self) -> Dict[str, ColumnInfo]:
  1542→        """Get column info including applied and calculated columns."""
  1543→        result = {}
  1544→        for col in self.df.columns:
  1545→            dtype = str(self.df[col].dtype)
  1546→            if dtype.startswith('datetime'):
  1547→                mtype = MTLType.TIMESTAMP
  1548→            elif dtype in ('int64', 'int32', 'int'):
  1549→                mtype = MTLType.INT
  1550→            elif dtype in ('float64', 'float32'):
  1551→                mtype = MTLType.FLOAT
  1552→            else:
  1553→                mtype = MTLType.STRING
  1554→            result[col] = ColumnInfo(name=col, mtype=mtype, pandas_dtype=dtype, is_physical=True)
  1555→        result.update(self._apply_columns)
  1556→        result.update(self._calculated_columns)
  1557→        return result
  1558→
  1559→    def execute(self, pipeline: Pipeline, all_schemas: Dict[str, Schema]) -> Any:
  1560→        """Execute the pipeline and return result."""
  1561→        # Resolve schema inheritance
  1562→        schema_fields = self.resolve_schema(pipeline.using_schema, all_schemas)
  1563→
  1564→        # Validate physical columns exist
  1565→        self.validate_physical_columns(schema_fields, self.df)
  1566→
  1567→        # Resolve parameter values
  1568→        resolved_params = self.resolve_param_values(pipeline.params)
  1569→
  1570→        # Build column info from schema
  1571→        # Add CSV columns and schema-level fields
  1572→        # Schema-level calculated fields are physical=False
  1573→
  1574→        # Phase 0: Add schema-level calculated columns to dataframe
  1575→        for field_name, field_def in schema_fields.items():
  1576→            if field_def.expr is not None:
  1577→                # Calculate this field
  1578→                col_info = self._eval_schema_field(field_name, field_def, resolved_params)
  1579→
  1580→        # Phase 1: Pipeline pre-stage calculated fields
  1581→        for calc in pipeline.calcs:
  1582→            if calc.stage == "pre":
  1583→                self._add_pipeline_calc_column(calc, resolved_params)
  1584→
  1585→        # Phase 2: Filters
  1586→        if pipeline.filters:
  1587→            mask = pd.Series([True] * len(self.df), index=self.df.index)
  1588→            for expr_str, parsed_ast in pipeline.filters:
  1589→                col_info = self.get_column_info()
  1590→                checker = TypeChecker(col_info, resolved_params, "pre")
  1591→                checker.check(parsed_ast)
  1592→                evaluator = ExpressionEvaluator(self.df, {n: v for n, (t, v) in resolved_params.items()})
  1593→                new_mask = pd.Series([True] * len(self.df), index=self.df.index)
  1594→                for i in range(len(self.df)):
  1595→                    try:
  1596→                        result = evaluator.evaluate(parsed_ast, i)
  1597→                        new_mask.iloc[i] = bool(result)
  1598→                    except (KeyError, TypeError, ZeroDivisionError) as e:
  1599→                        new_mask.iloc[i] = False
  1600→                mask = mask & new_mask
  1601→            self.df = self.df[mask].reset_index(drop=True)
  1602→
  1603→        # Phase 3: Apply transforms (in order)
  1604→        for expr_str, new_col, parsed_ast in pipeline.applies:
  1605→            self._apply_transform(expr_str, new_col, parsed_ast, resolved_params)
  1606→
  1607→        # Phase 4: Windowing
  1608→        if pipeline.window:
  1609→            self._apply_window(pipeline.window)
  1610→
  1611→        # Phase 5: Group by
  1612→        if pipeline.group_by:
  1613→            self._apply_group_by(pipeline.group_by)
  1614→
  1615→        # Phase 6: Aggregation
  1616→        result = self._apply_aggregates(pipeline.aggregates, resolved_params)
  1617→
  1618→        # Phase 7: Post-aggregation calculated fields
  1619→        if any(c.stage == "post_agg" for c in pipeline.calcs):
  1620→            post_agg_calcs = [c for c in pipeline.calcs if c.stage == "post_agg"]
  1621→            # Validate post-agg calcs can reference available fields
  1622→            available_fields = set()
  1623→            if hasattr(self, '_window_applied') and self._window_applied:
  1624→                available_fields.add('window_start')
  1625→                available_fields.add('window_end')
  1626→            if hasattr(self, '_group_columns'):
  1627→                available_fields.update(self._group_columns)
  1628→            available_fields.update([a.alias for a in pipeline.aggregates])
  1629→
  1630→            for calc in post_agg_calcs:
  1631→                # Type checking happens during evaluation
  1632→                pass
  1633→
  1634→            # Apply post-agg calcs
  1635→            evaluator = PostAggEvaluator(result, {n: v for n, (t, v) in resolved_params.items()}, post_agg_calcs)
  1636→            result = evaluator.evaluate_all()
  1637→
  1638→        # Phase 8: Format output
  1639→        return self._format_output(result, pipeline)

  1641→    def _eval_schema_field(self, field_name: str, field_def: SchemaField, resolved_params: Dict[str, Tuple[MTLType, Any]]):
  1642→        """Evaluate a schema-level calculated field."""
  1643→        # Parse and type check the expression
  1644→        parser = DSLParser("")  # Dummy parser for preprocessing
  1645→        processed = parser._preprocess_general_expr(field_def.expr, "pre")
  1646→        parsed_ast = parser._safe_parse(processed, mode='eval')
  1647→
  1648→        col_info = self.get_column_info()
  1649→        checker = TypeChecker(col_info, resolved_params, "pre")
  1650→        result_type = checker.check(parsed_ast)
  1651→
  1652→        if result_type != field_def.mtype:
  1653→            raise TypeError(f"Schema calc {field_name} type mismatch: {result_type} vs {field_def.mtype}")
  1654→
  1655→        # Evaluate and add to dataframe
  1656→        values = []
  1657→        evaluator = ExpressionEvaluator(self.df, {n: v for n, (t, v) in resolved_params.items()})
  1658→        for i in range(len(self.df)):
  1659→            try:
  1660→                val = evaluator.evaluate(parsed_ast, i)
  1661→                values.append(val)
  1662→            except Exception:
  1663→                values.append(None)
  1664→
  1665→        dtype_map = {
  1666→            MTLType.INT: 'int64',
  1667→            MTLType.FLOAT: 'float64',
  1668→            MTLType.TIMESTAMP: 'datetime64[ns]',
  1669→            MTLType.BOOL: 'bool',
  1670→            MTLType.STRING: 'object',
  1671→        }
  1672→        dtype = dtype_map.get(result_type, 'object')
  1673→        self.df[field_name] = pd.Series(values, dtype=dtype)
  1674→        self._calculated_columns[field_name] = ColumnInfo(
  1675→            name=field_name,
  1676→            mtype=result_type,
  1677→            pandas_dtype=str(self.df[field_name].dtype),
  1678→            is_physical=False
  1679→        )

  1681→    def _add_pipeline_calc_column(self, calc: CalcInfo, resolved_params: Dict[str, Tuple[MTLType, Any]]):
  1682→        """Add a pipeline pre-stage calculated column."""
  1683→        # Already parsed, just evaluate
  1684→        col_info = self.get_column_info()
  1685→        checker = TypeChecker(col_info, resolved_params, "pre")
  1686→        result_type = checker.check(calc.parsed_ast)
  1687→
  1688→        if result_type != calc.mtype:
  1689→            raise TypeError(f"Calc {calc.name} type mismatch: {result_type} vs {calc.mtype}")
  1690→
  1691→        if calc.name in self._apply_columns or calc.name in self._calculated_columns:
  1692→            raise SyntaxError(f"Duplicate calc name: {calc.name}")
  1693→
  1694→        values = []
  1695→        evaluator = ExpressionEvaluator(self.df, {n: v for n, (t, v) in resolved_params.items()})
  1696→        for i in range(len(self.df)):
  1697→            try:
  1698→                val = evaluator.evaluate(calc.parsed_ast, i)
  1699→                values.append(val)
  1700→            except Exception:
  1701→                values.append(None)
  1702→
  1703→        dtype_map = {
  1704→            MTLType.INT: 'int64',
  1705→            MTLType.FLOAT: 'float64',
  1706→            MTLType.TIMESTAMP: 'datetime64[ns]',
  1707→            MTLType.BOOL: 'bool',
  1708→            MTLType.STRING: 'object',
  1709→        }
  1710→        dtype = dtype_map.get(result_type, 'object')
  1711→        self.df[calc.name] = pd.Series(values, dtype=dtype)
  1712→        self._calculated_columns[calc.name] = ColumnInfo(
  1713→            name=calc.name,
  1714→            mtype=result_type,
  1715→            pandas_dtype=str(self.df[calc.name].dtype),
  1716→            is_physical=False
  1717→        )

  1719→    def _apply_transform(self, expr_str: str, new_col: str, parsed_ast: ast.AST, resolved_params: Dict[str, Tuple[MTLType, Any]]):
  1720→        """Apply a transform expression to create a new column."""
  1721→        col_info = self.get_column_info()
  1722→        checker = TypeChecker(col_info, resolved_params, "pre")
  1723→        result_type = checker.check(parsed_ast)
  1724→
  1725→        values = []
  1726→        evaluator = ExpressionEvaluator(self.df, {n: v for n, (t, v) in resolved_params.items()})
  1727→        for i in range(len(self.df)):
  1728→            try:
  1729→                val = evaluator.evaluate(parsed_ast, i)
  1730→                values.append(val)
  1731→            except Exception:
  1732→                values.append(None)
  1733→
  1734→        dtype_map = {
  1735→            MTLType.INT: 'int64',
  1736→            MTLType.FLOAT: 'float64',
  1737→            MTLType.TIMESTAMP: 'datetime64[ns]',
  1738→            MTLType.BOOL: 'bool',
  1739→            MTLType.STRING: 'object',
  1740→        }
  1741→        dtype = dtype_map.get(result_type, 'object')
  1742→        self.df[new_col] = pd.Series(values, dtype=dtype)
  1743→        self._apply_columns[new_col] = ColumnInfo(

  1749→    def _apply_window(self, window: WindowSpec):
  1750→        """Apply windowing - add window_start and window_end columns."""
  1751→        epoch = pd.Timestamp('1970-01-01T00:00:00Z', tz='UTC')
  1752→        ts_ns = (self.df['timestamp'] - epoch).dt.total_seconds() * 1_000_000_000
  1753→        window_ns = window.duration.total_seconds() * 1_000_000_000
  1754→        window_idx = (ts_ns // window_ns).astype(int)
  1755→        self.df['window_start'] = epoch + pd.to_timedelta(window_idx * window_ns, unit='ns')
  1756→        self.df['window_end'] = self.df['window_start'] + pd.to_timedelta(window_ns, unit='ns')
  1757→        self._window_applied = True
  1758→
  1759→    def _apply_group_by(self, group: GroupSpec):
  1760→        """Apply group by - set index."""
  1761→        self._group_applied = True
  1762→        self._group_columns = group.columns
  1763→
  1764→    def _apply_aggregates(self, aggregates: List[Aggregation], resolved_params: Dict[str, Tuple[MTLType, Any]]) -> Union[Dict, List]:
  1765→        """Apply aggregation and return result."""
  1766→        has_window = hasattr(self, '_window_applied') and self._window_applied
  1767→        has_group = hasattr(self, '_group_applied') and self._group_applied
  1768→
  1769→        if has_window or has_group:
  1770→            return self._aggregate_grouped_or_windowed(aggregates, resolved_params)
  1771→        else:
  1772→            return self._aggregate_flat(aggregates)
  1773→
  1774→    def _aggregate_flat(self, aggregates: List[Aggregation]) -> Dict:
  1775→        """Aggregate without grouping or windowing."""
  1776→        result = {}
  1777→        for agg in aggregates:
  1778→            if agg.column == '*':
  1779→                result[agg.alias] = len(self.df)
  1780→            else:
  1781→                if agg.column not in self.df.columns:
  1782→                    raise ValueError(f"Column not found: {agg.column}")
  1783→                col_data = self.df[agg.column]
  1784→                if not pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(col_data):
  1785→                    raise TypeError(f"Aggregate requires numeric column: {agg.column}")
  1786→                col_data = col_data.dropna()
  1787→                if len(col_data) == 0:
  1788→                    result[agg.alias] = None
  1789→                else:
  1790→                    result[agg.alias] = self._compute_agg(agg.fn, col_data)
  1791→        return result
  1792→
  1793→    def _compute_agg(self, fn: str, data: pd.Series) -> float:
  1794→        """Compute single aggregation."""
  1795→        if fn == 'sum':
  1796→            return float(data.sum())
  1797→        elif fn == 'average':
  1798→            return float(data.mean())
  1799→        elif fn == 'median':
  1800→            return float(data.median())
  1801→        elif fn == 'count':
  1802→            return float(len(data))
  1803→        elif fn == 'std':
  1804→            return float(data.std(ddof=0))
  1805→        elif fn == 'var':
  1806→            return float(data.var(ddof=0))
  1807→        elif fn == 'min':
  1808→            return float(data.min())
  1809→        elif fn == 'max':
  1810→            return float(data.max())
  1811→        else:
  1812→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown aggregate: {fn}")
  1813→
  1814→    def _aggregate_grouped_or_windowed(self, aggregates: List[Aggregation], resolved_params: Dict[str, Tuple[MTLType, Any]]) -> List[Dict]:
  1815→        """Unified aggregation for grouped, windowed, or both cases."""
  1816→        group_cols = getattr(self, '_group_columns', [])
  1817→        has_window = hasattr(self, '_window_applied') and self._window_applied
  1818→
  1819→        if has_window:
  1820→            group_keys = ['window_start', 'window_end'] + group_cols
  1821→        else:
  1822→            group_keys = group_cols
  1823→
  1824→        results = []
  1825→        grouped = self.df.groupby(group_keys, sort=False)
  1826→
  1827→        for keys, group in grouped:
  1828→            result = {}
  1829→
  1830→            if has_window:
  1831→                result['window_start'] = self._format_timestamp(keys[0])
  1832→                result['window_end'] = self._format_timestamp(keys[1])
  1833→                key_offset = 2
  1834→            else:
  1835→                key_offset = 0
  1836→
  1837→            if group_cols:
  1838→                group_keys_part = keys[key_offset:]
  1839→                if len(group_cols) == 1:
  1840→                    result[group_cols[0]] = group_keys_part
  1841→                else:
  1842→                    for i, col in enumerate(group_cols):
  1843→                        result[col] = group_keys_part[i]
  1844→
  1845→            for agg in aggregates:
  1846→                if agg.column == '*':
  1847→                    result[agg.alias] = len(group)
  1848→                else:
  1849→                    if agg.column not in group.columns:
  1850→                        result[agg.alias] = None
  1851→                        continue
  1852→                    col_data = group[agg.column].dropna()
  1853→                    if len(col_data) == 0:
  1854→                        result[agg.alias] = None
  1855→                    else:
  1856→                        result[agg.alias] = self._compute_agg(agg.fn, col_data)
  1857→
  1858→            results.append(result)
  1859→
  1860→        return results
  1861→
  1862→    def _format_timestamp(self, ts) -> str:
  1863→        """Format timestamp for output."""
  1864→        return ts.strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ') if hasattr(ts, 'strftime') else str(ts)
  1865→
  1866→    def _format_output(self, result: Any, pipeline: Pipeline) -> str:
  1867→        """Format output according to determinism rules."""
  1868→        import json
  1869→
  1870→        has_window = hasattr(self, '_window_applied') and self._window_applied
  1871→        has_group = hasattr(self, '_group_applied') and self._group_applied
  1872→        post_agg_calcs = [c.name for c in pipeline.calcs if c.stage == 'post_agg']
  1873→
  1874→        if isinstance(result, dict):
  1875→            return json.dumps(result, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'))
  1876→
  1877→        elif isinstance(result, list):
  1878→            # Sort by window_start then group keys
  1879→            def sort_key(item):
  1880→                keys = []
  1881→                if has_window:
  1882→                    keys.append(item.get('window_start', ''))
  1883→                if has_group:
  1884→                    for col in getattr(self, '_group_columns', []):
  1885→                        keys.append(str(item.get(col, '')))
  1886→                return tuple(keys)
  1887→
  1888→            result.sort(key=sort_key)
  1889→
  1890→            def sort_object_keys(obj):
  1891→                if isinstance(obj, dict):
  1892→                    fixed_keys = []
  1893→                    if has_window:
  1894→                        fixed_keys = ['window_start', 'window_end']
  1895→                    if has_group:
  1896→                        fixed_keys.extend(getattr(self, '_group_columns', []))
  1897→                    # Agg aliases sorted lexicographically
  1898→                    agg_aliases = sorted([a.alias for a in pipeline.aggregates])
  1899→                    # Post-agg calc names sorted lexicographically
  1900→                    calc_aliases = sorted(post_agg_calcs)
  1901→                    new_obj = {}
  1902→                    for k in fixed_keys:
  1903→                        if k in obj:
  1904→                            new_obj[k] = sort_object_keys(obj[k])
  1905→                    for k in agg_aliases:
  1906→                        if k in obj:
  1907→                            new_obj[k] = sort_object_keys(obj[k])
  1908→                    for k in calc_aliases:
  1909→                        if k in obj:
  1910→                            new_obj[k] = sort_object_keys(obj[k])
  1911→                    return new_obj
  1912→                elif isinstance(obj, list):
  1913→                    return [sort_object_keys(i) for i in obj]
  1914→                else:
  1915→                    return obj
  1916→
  1917→            result = [sort_object_keys(item) for item in result]
  1918→            return json.dumps(result, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'))
  1919→
  1920→        raise TypeError(f"Unexpected result type: {type(result)}")
  1921→

  1925→def parse_args():
  1926→    import argparse
  1927→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='MTL: Market Transformation Language CLI')
  1928→    parser.add_argument('--csv', required=True, help='Path to input CSV file')
  1929→    parser.add_argument('--dsl', required=True, help='Path to DSL file, or - for STDIN')
  1930→    parser.add_argument('--out', default=None, help='Output file path (default: STDOUT)')
  1931→    parser.add_argument('--tz', default='UTC', help='Default timezone (default: UTC)')
